pi_items.source: Code analysis of setup_task.sh - creates 5 finding sections (Access Control Deficiencies, Segregation of Duties Gaps, Vendor Due Diligence Failures, Data Retention Policy Violations, IT Change Management Weaknesses)
setup_task.sh lines 143, 180, 206, 226, 245 - exact titles used in section_head() calls
setup_task.sh lines 104-110 rows_data list and corresponding plain() calls for each finding
Task description and verifier.py line 165-174 confirm footer should contain 'Internal Audit Report' text; setup_task.sh does not create footer (agent task is to add it)
setup_task.sh line 288 saves to this path; task.json references this path; verifier expects to find audit_final.docx as output
task.json and verifier.py line 204 expect output at this path; export_result.sh line 14 checks for this file

pi_items.metadata_value: IIA Standard 2400 (Communicating Results), 2410 (Criteria for Communicating), 2200 (Engagement Planning), 2300 (Performing the Engagement), 2060 (Reporting to Senior Management and the Board), 2120 (Risk Management), 2500 (Monitoring Progress)
pi_items.verified_value: These are real International Standards for the Professional Practice of Internal Auditing (IPPF) issued by the Institute of Internal Auditors (IIA). Standards 2400 series and others exist and are mandatory guidance.
pi_items.source: Web search verified IIA Standard 2400 exists as 'Communicating Results' and is part of the mandatory guidance in IPPF. The 2024 IPPF structure uses different naming but historical 2000-series standards referenced in task are real.

pi_items.metadata_value: FFIEC IT Examination Handbook (Information Security Booklet, Audit Booklet), NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5 AC-2, COSO Integrated Framework (2013) Principle 10, Sarbanes-Oxley Act §302, SEC 17a-4, COBIT 5, ITIL change management
pi_items.verified_value: All referenced frameworks and standards are real and exist: FFIEC IT Handbook published by Federal Financial Institutions Examination Council; NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5 AC-2 is real security control; COSO 2013 Framework Principle 10 addresses Control Activities including segregation of duties; SOX §302 requires CEO/CFO certification; SEC Rule 17a-4 governs electronic records retention; COBIT 5 is IT governance framework by ISACA; ITIL provides IT service management best practices.
pi_items.source: Web searches confirmed all frameworks are real and authoritative: https://www.theiia.org/en/standards/ for IIA, https://ithandbook.ffiec.gov/ for FFIEC, https://csrc.nist.gov for NIST, https://www.coso.org for COSO, https://www.sec.gov for SEC Rule 17a-4, https://www.isaca.org/resources/cobit for COBIT

pi_items.metadata_value: FiServ Premier core banking platform, Oracle Financial Cloud ERP (Accounts Payable module), Workday (HR system), ServiceNow (change management workflows)
pi_items.verified_value: All are real enterprise software systems: FiServ Premier is widely-used core banking platform used by U.S. financial institutions; Oracle Financials Cloud is cloud ERP with AP module; Workday is cloud HCM used by 60%+ of Fortune 500; ServiceNow is ITSM platform.
pi_items.source: Web searches confirmed: https://www.fiserv.com/premier for FiServ, https://www.oracle.com/erp/financials/ for Oracle, https://www.workday.com for Workday, https://www.servicenow.com/products/change-management.html for ServiceNow

pi_items.verified_value: NorthBridge Financial Group / Northbridge Financial Group names exist in reality but are used fictionally in this task. Real entities with these names include: Northbridge Financial Group (Florida wealth management), Northbridge Financial Corporation (Canadian insurance), Northbridge Financial (private family office), and others.
pi_items.source: Web search found multiple real companies with 'Northbridge Financial Group' or 'NorthBridge Financial Group' names, but the specific Q3 2024 internal audit scenario is fictional.

privileged_info_summary: This task creates a synthetic Q3 2024 Internal Audit Report for NorthBridge Financial Group containing five findings: Access Control Deficiencies (High), Segregation of Duties Gaps (High), Vendor Due Diligence Failures (Medium), Data Retention Policy Violations (Low), and IT Change Management Weaknesses (Medium). The task document references legitimate regulatory frameworks including IIA Standards (2400 Communicating Results, 2200 Engagement Planning, etc.), FFIEC IT Examination Handbook, NIST SP 800-53, COSO Integrated Framework 2013 Principle 10, Sarbanes-Oxley Act §302, SEC Rule 17a-4, COBIT 5, and ITIL best practices. The task references real enterprise systems used by financial institutions: FiServ Premier (core banking), Oracle Financial Cloud (ERP with accounts payable), Workday (HR), and ServiceNow (IT change management). All regulatory references and system names are authentic; the audit report content and specific findings are synthetic.
